mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk-taking, so declines in policy rates over the prior month predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in most triggered samples, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates faster than the prior three-month trend, real rate expectations and future Fed restraint rise, pressuring equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation accelerations are not followed by negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the signal loses sign consistency after controlling for the level of FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth signals labor-market deterioration, which leads investors to price weaker consumer demand and lower corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if labor deterioration triggers do not coincide with below-zero 20-day returns, or if the two-variable interaction adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in RSAFS indicates weakening retail demand and inventory caution, which transmits to lower near-term earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS growth is not followed by negative 5-day returns in a majority of occurrences, or if the effect disappears after one month of forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST also rises, the economy is expanding in a supply-healthy manner that reduces recession odds and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent gains in industrial production and housing starts do not precede positive 60-day returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction term is not positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Stable or falling UNRATE during a tightening cycle indicates the economy is absorbing higher rates without material labor damage, which sustains earnings confidence and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if elevated policy-rate periods with non-rising unemployment do not produce positive 20-day returns, or if the signal flips negative when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and RSAFS is also expanding, aggregate demand is strengthening across both production and spending, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail-sales growth does not predict positive 60-day returns, or if the combined signal is weaker than either component alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving capacity utilization and better earnings leverage, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change turns positive after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if industrial production rebounds after a slowdown are not followed by positive 5-day returns more often than not, or if the effect is confined to a single short sample window.
